purpose: Proof-of-concept for instance-authored compression plans. Tags reads/tool-uses with purpose and state so compress-reads can apply discernment beyond size-thresholding.

purpose.orientation: Reading setup/config/arrival docs; shapes downstream work; compress lightly.
purpose.ingest: Content itself is input to current reasoning; preserve texture and specific quotes.
purpose.scan: Reading to find something specific; post-hoc, only the found thing matters.
purpose.pre-edit: Reading to understand what to modify; once Edit happens, Edit captures the region.
purpose.verify: Reading/running after writing to check work; finding is in subsequent assistant text.
purpose.explore: Cascade of listing/glob/ls; individually low-value, collectively a 'looked around here'.
purpose.commit: Edit or Write tool_use whose content is now captured in file-on-disk.

state.live: Still informing current work; skip compression regardless of size.
state.superseded: Content captured by a subsequent tool use (Edit captured the region; Write committed the file; verification finding captured in assistant text).
state.stale: Was active; work has moved on; safe to compress normally.

compression.pointer-only: Replace content with a one-line pointer (tool + target + state). Use for commits and superseded scans/verifies.
compression.head-tail: Preserve first ~300 + last ~300 chars so the read is still recognizable. Use for superseded pre-edit reads.
compression.summarize: Route to a summarizer (deterministic or delegated) with purpose as context.

group_notes.description: Edit to render_summary_template adding stone-fragment scratch section + fix to extract_summary_body short-circuit bug.
group_notes.outcome: Committed to session_memory.py. Tests passed. All Reads in this group were pre-edit and are superseded by the Edit and by the final file state on disk.

group_notes.description: Extracted TOPIC_RULES, keyword pools, and STRONG_BOUNDARY_PHRASES to session_memory_taxonomy.py. Added 4 new topics (arrival, sensory-stones, practice, loop-and-trust) with explicit tier rules.
group_notes.outcome: New file written; session_memory.py updated to import from it. End-to-end test on real session (0704f048) classified 'practice' segments that previously fell to 'general'.

group_notes.description: Edit to _summarize_tool_content_for_compression adding head/tail preservation and tool-type-aware labels.
group_notes.outcome: Committed to session_memory.py. Dry-run on this session identified 14 records; this plan describes how to compress them.

summary.observation: All 14 records are superseded. This session was tightly scoped to code refinement: every Read was pre-edit, every Edit/Write committed to disk, the one Bash was verification. Classic 'clean' session for aggressive compression. A session with ingest reads (stones, threads, letters) would have more live/stale entries and benefit more from the plan-driven approach where texture preservation matters.
suggested_followups: Extend cmd_compress_reads with a --plan flag that consumes this schema.
Add a 'pointer-only' compression mode (even shorter than current head/tail) for superseded commits.
Consider whether the plan should travel alongside the compressed session as a sidecar, so future instances reading this session see not just what was compressed but why.
Over time, live/stale transitions can be made manually or detected heuristically (e.g., 'no references to file X in last N turns → bump associated reads from live to stale').
